title: Indiscriminate and unlawful attacks on Aleppo
pages_or_sections: Investigation summary for the first half of August 2012
strengths: Provides a bounded floor of documented civilian deaths in investigated attacks and qualitative perpetrator attribution context.
limitations: The report says the overwhelming majority of investigated cases were government attacks, but does not support assigning every documented death in the sample to one actor with clean numeric precision.
notes: Retained wording: around 30 attacks investigated; more than 80 civilians killed; overwhelming majority killed or injured in attacks by government forces.

title: Implementation of Security Council resolutions 2139, 2165 and 2191 (selected Aleppo passages)
pages_or_sections: Aleppo situation summary including last week of September 2016 and population under siege
date_scope: Late September 2016 with siege-population context in October 2016
strengths: Provides a clean eastern-Aleppo siege population figure and short-window fatality floors on both east and west sides of the city.
limitations: The >300 and >80 figures are for 'people', not fully status-classified victims, and the source attributes them mainly to airstrikes without a complete actor-separation in the retained text.
notes: Retained summary wording: more than 300 people killed in eastern Aleppo in the last week of September 2016; more than 80 killed in western Aleppo; an estimated 275,000 reside in eastern Aleppo.

title: Russia/Syria: War Crimes in Month of Bombing Aleppo
pages_or_sections: Opening summary and example 27 September 2016 attack
strengths: Explicitly identifies the Russian-Syrian coalition as the attacking force in a month-long aerial campaign and gives a specific civilian-death event.
limitations: Single-event numeric evidence does not provide a full campaign total.
notes: Retained wording: Russian-Syrian coalition committed war crimes during the bombing campaign; one aerial attack killed at least 24 civilians on 27 September 2016.
